input.feature: Approval node decision persistence across resume
input.task: Create a workflow with an <Approval> node that requests a ship/no-ship decision. On denial, set onDeny='continue' and branch: if approved, run 'release' task; if denied, run 'schedule-followup' task. Start the workflow and let it block at the approval. Use smithers ps to see waiting-approval. Deny the approval via smithers deny RUN_ID --node APPROVAL_NODE_ID --by alice. Resume with smithers up workflow.tsx --resume RUN_ID and verify the 'schedule-followup' branch executes while 'release' is skipped. Then start a new run, approve it, and verify 'release' executes. Show both smithers deny and smithers approve commands and the resulting branch execution.

verify.rubric: Judge must verify: (1) Approval node with mode='approve' (default); (2) request object with title and summary; (3) onDeny='continue' so workflow proceeds after denial; (4) ternary or <Branch to select downstream task based on approved boolean; (5) first run: reach approval, use smithers deny to deny it, smithers ps shows waiting-approval, approve command succeeds, resume continues to schedule-followup branch; (6) second run: reach approval, use smithers approve, resume continues to release branch; (7) output schema includes which branch was taken. Candidate must show actual CLI command calls and results from two complete flows (denied and approved).

metadata.feature: Approval node decision persistence across resume

input.feature: Approval node decision persistence across resume

On denial, set onDeny='continue' and branch: if approved, run 'release' task; if denied, run 'schedule-followup' task. Start the workflow and let it block at the approval. Use smithers ps to see waiting-approval. Deny the approval via smithers deny RUN_ID --node APPROVAL_NODE_ID --by alice. Resume with smithers up workflow.tsx --resume RUN_ID and verify the 'schedule-followup' branch executes while 'release' is skipped. Then start a new run, approve it, and verify 'release' executes. Show both smithers deny and smithers approve commands and the resulting branch execution.

verify.rubric: Judge must verify: (1) Approval node with mode='approve' (default); (2) request object with title and summary; (3) onDeny='continue' so workflow proceeds after denial; (4) ternary or <Branch to select downstream task based on approved boolean; (5) first run: reach approval, use smithers deny to deny it, smithers ps shows waiting-approval, approve command succeeds, resume continues to schedule-followup branch; (6) second run: reach approval, use smithers approve, resume continues to release branch; (7) output schema includes which branch was taken. Candidate must show actual CLI command calls and results from two complete flows (denied and approved).

metadata.feature: Approval node decision persistence across resume
